properties.remark: EM REPORTS RAPID RISES ALONG MIDDLE LOUP RIVER NW OF ROCKVILLE, POTENTIALLY AS MUCH AS 4FT HIGHER IN 20 MIN. CATTLE ARE IN DANGER.

*** 1 INJ *** TWO INSTANCES OF VEHICLES SWEPT INTO WOOD RIVER IN VICINITY OF 17TH AVE AND 92ND ST REQUIRING WATER RESCUES. ONE PERSON TRANSPORTED TO HOSPITAL. 5 OTHER WATER RESCUES THROUGHOUT THE COUNTY BETWEEN 9PM AND MIDNIGHT LOCAL TIME. TIME OF THIS REPORT REPRESENTS TIME OF INCIDENT INVOLVING THE INJURY.

LOUP RIVER HAS OVERTOPPED THE INTAKE STRUCTURE AT LOUP POWER DIVERSION. CANAL MAY HAVE ALSO BREACHED SOMEWHERE NEAR HWY 22 BRIDGE JUST WEST OF THE INTERSECTION OF HWY 22 AND HWY 39. EVACUATIONS UNDERWAY IN SOUTH SIDE OF CITY OF GENOA.

THIS REPORT IS A CORRECTION TO EARLIER LSR ISSUED FOR 9S SPEED AT 507 PM. BASED ON ADDITIONAL PHOTO EVIDENCE THIS BRIEF TORNADO ACTUALLY TOUCHED DOWN NEAR THIS APPROXIMATE LOCATION. NO DAMAGE REPORTED.

AFTER VIEWING FURTHER PHOTOGRAPHIC EVIDENCE, THE LOCATION OF THIS REPORT APPEARS TO BE TOO FAR NORTHEAST. PLEASE SEE MODIFIED LOCATION IN SEPARATE TORNADO LSR VALID 450 PM FOUR MILES NORTHWEST OF WEBSTER STATE PARK.

LAW ENFORCEMENT AND SPOTTERS REPORTED ONE FARMSTEAD HAD OUTBUILDING AND POWER LINE DAMAGE. ANOTHER FARMSTEAD TO ITS WEST HAD NOTABLE TREE DAMAGE. THERE WAS ALSO AT LEAST ONE PIVOT OVERTURNED. TIME IS ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.

EMERGENCY MANAGER REPORTED WINDS OF AT LEAST 50 MPH IN PLAINVILLE FROM 800-820 PM. NUMEROUS TREE LIMBS DOWN AROUND TOWN, OF UNKNOWN DIAMETER. A CARPORT WAS DAMAGED ON THE SOUTH SIDE OF TOWN.
SPOTTER REPORTED GUSTY WINDS FROM APPROXIMATELY 7:45 PM THROUGH 8:20 PM, PEAKING WITH AN ESTIMATED 60 MPH GUST AROUND 8:10 PM. PHOTO SHOWED TREE DAMAGE IN TOWN. REPORT WAS VIA FACEBOOK.

3-5 INCHES OF RAIN FELL BEAVER CITY TO ORLEANS LAST EVENING AND NIGHT. 18 INCHES OF WATER FLOWING DOWN BROWN AVE TO HARLAN COUNTY LAKE. SOME COUNTY GRAVEL ROADS WASHED OUT, INCLUDING SOME THAT HAVE NOT WASHED OUT IN DECADES. ROADS THAT WERE RECENTLY REPAIRED FROM MARCH 2019 FLOODING HAVE BEEN DAMAGED AGAIN. TIME ESTIMATED.
VIDEO RCVD VIA SOCIAL MEDIA OF 6 FT CULVERT WASHED OUT AND GRAVEL ROAD COMPLETELY GONE ON 710 RD JUST N OF REPUBLICAN CITY. EXACT TIME UNKNOWN.

BRIEF TOUCHDOWN. EF-0. ONE HOME IMPACTED. SMALL PORTION OF ROOF PEALED OFF. ATTIC INSULATION PLASTERED ONTO HOME AND PICKUP TRUCK PARKED OUTSIDE. A SMALL NUMBER OF PLANKS OF HARDIE BOARD SIDING RIPPED OFF. TWO SMALL TRAILERS TURNED. A SMALL PIECE OF SHINGLE WEDGED INTO A PIECE OF FARM EQUIPMENT. A PIECE OF STRAW WEDGED IN THE MIDDLE BETWEEN TWO WINDOWS THAT SLIDE HORIZONTALLY. TIME EST FM RADAR BUT IS HIGHLY UNCERTAIN GVN LACK OF DECENT SIGNATURES.

DELAYED REPORT. TREMENDOUS AMOUNTS OF MOSTLY SMALL HAIL. DRIFTS OF HAIL STILL EXISTED AT SUNRISE ON THE MRNG OF THE 21ST. ONE FARMER REPORTED 800-900 ACRES OF CORN WERE DESTROYED NE-E-SE OF TOWN. CORN WAS STRIPPED LEAVING JUST THE STALKS. WHEAT THAT WAS NOT FLATTENED WAS ALSO DAMAGED. SOME QUARTER SIZE HAILSTONES WERE REPORTED IN TOWN.
